next_step_text: 6/16 - Amanda Moir who reports directly to new CPO Helen Whitehouse is scheduled to attend June 25th Toronto event. Have EM'd and WhatsApp'd Ludovic with no response to date. Inviting Amanda and Helen to Horizon in Sept. 4/25MF - no response to our reach outs. Invited a number of McCain procurement titles to come to our booth at Gartner May 4-6 in Orlando. Working to get a live call set with Hacke

recent_activities_14d.subject: 6/25MF - Amanda Moir attended Toronto RT event and had good questions around AI deployment and AgenticAI use cases which

buyer_dependent_items.text: Provide the incoming CPO's approval of the analytics strategy so the paused POC can restart

competitive_position.summary: The strongest current threat is Do Nothing / organizational inertia: an ~8-month internal reorg has frozen the deal and the buyer paused it himself on 24 Oct 2025. A formal multi-vendor evaluation is anticipated ('it might be 6 other suppliers', Alexa Bradley, 10 Dec 2025) but no rival is named in current evidence. Coupa, SpendHQ and an in-house/manual approach remain on record from prior sweeps with no fresh mention this run, carried as lower-priority historical context.

competitors.name: Do Nothing / Organizational Inertia
competitors.quote: I proposed and it has been accepted to put things on hold... none of us is here to lose time.
competitors.how_we_win: Quantify the cost of delay and use the Merlin/Agentic AI analytics 'first look' to give the incoming CPO a reason to act now; create a dated forcing function via the reconnect.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.source: living memory (prior sweeps; not re-mentioned this run)
competitors.how_we_win: Lead with Merlin/Agentic AI and analytics depth on intake and tail spend where Zycus differentiates.

competitors.name: SpendHQ
competitors.source: living memory (prior sweeps; not re-mentioned this run)
competitors.how_we_win: Position iAnalyze plus the full S2C suite against a point analytics tool.

competitors.name: In-house / manual approach
competitors.how_we_win: Show automation and taxonomy-accuracy gains from a scoped POC versus manual effort.

champion_strength.source: Avoma calls 24 Oct 2025 & 13 Feb 2026 (Ludovic Verdelet); SF Next_Step__c 16 Jun 2026
champion_strength.summary: Ludovic Verdelet is a genuine, engaged advocate who has carried the relationship and pushed the POC internally, but he is single-threaded, personally at risk in the reorg, and has recently gone quiet on rep outreach. Amanda Moir is an emerging second thread but not yet a champion.

meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is identified by name and title but not engaged. Helen Whitehouse (Chief Procurement Officer, an internal promotion from the relevant stakeholder) is the new CPO who must re-approve the analytics strategy before the POC restarts, per Ludovic on 24 Oct 2025: 'I'd rather have this strategy approved by Ellen right now because she will be the one for the years to come.' Salesforce Next Step (16 Jun 2026) confirms she owns approval and that Zycus is trying to reach her through a Sept Horizon invite. She has never had a direct working conversation with Zycus, so commercial appetite and view of the analytics strategy are unknown; securing a direct COE alignment session is the gating move.

recommended_moves: Book a working session with Amanda Moir on Intake/Orchestration and ANA quick wins.
Reach Ludovic through a fresh channel or via Amanda to confirm CPO approval status.
Secure a direct alignment session with Helen Whitehouse or the procurement COE.
